message:
Stage 0.5 (typed) D0.R escalated on round 0 (before cap) — the directed revise cannot fix the findings in place: scale_drop@thm:tv-envelope-design requires a statement-level rewrite, not an in-place proof/prose edit. The current theorem sentence names the schedule-level minimax value as `M_{beta,k,q} = inf_{p in S_{k,q}} A_beta(p)` immediately after stating fixed-schedule variance in scaled units, so the honest fix is to rewrite the asserted sentence to distinguish the dimensionless amplification value `M_{beta,k,q}` from the actual envelope-variance value `(sigma_0^2/n) M_{beta,k,q}` over schedules. Under the HARD LIMIT, changing a theorem statement/conclusion is orchestrator-owned via the proto and any core-only edit would be discarded on re-solve. The remaining findings are in-place-fixable, but the spec requires early escalation rather than a partial patch once any directed finding needs a statement-level change.

message:
Stage 0.5 (typed) D0.R escalated on round 0 (before cap) — the directed revise cannot fix the findings in place: Cannot resolve all findings with an in-place Stage 0.R core edit under the hard statement lock. `oeq:exact-nested-minimax` (`wrong_support`) requires changing the OEQ statement itself: it currently says Chebyshev exact-risk rate-feasibility is 'by lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper', but the honest support is `lem:exact-chebyshev-rate-feasible` (or `thm:chebyshev-minimax` plus the bridge). `lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper` (`redundant_class_restriction`) is also statement-level: the lemma is stated over `P_beta` even though its proof only consumes `ass:round-mean-variance-envelope`; the honest fix is to broaden/restatethe lemma or change the exact-risk object/class in the proto. Those are orchestrator-owned statement/proto changes, so a partial in-place edit here would be hollow.
